topic: A person finds out their biography was written without their knowledge
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elias traced the embossed lettering on the document, the ink cool against his fingertip. The words scrolled by—his childhood dreams, his supposed triumphs, the quiet, almost clinical descriptions of his life. A knot tightened in his chest, a cold, heavy pressure that made his breath catch in his throat. He reread the passage about his pivotal decision in college, the narrative so polished, so utterly devoid of the messy, uncertain reality he remembered. He pushed the paper away, the sound sharp in the quiet study. Every room felt too small, the familiar furniture suddenly alien, as if the very architecture of his existence had been rearranged by an unseen hand. He stood by the window, watching the city lights blur, feeling utterly anchored to the spot, unable to move toward anything new.

[story 2]

"This isn't me," she whispered, the sound brittle in the cavernous silence of the attic. Clara held the manuscript, the pages smelling faintly of aged paper and someone else's ambition. Her hands trembled, not from cold, but from a profound disorientation. She looked at the photographs embedded within the text—images of milestones she’d never experienced. A strange numbness spread through her limbs, making it difficult to lift her arms or even blink with full intent. She sank onto the dusty floorboards, pulling her knees to her chest. The air seemed thick, suffocating, and she found herself staring blankly at a cobweb, the energy to even summon a single tear completely depleted.

[story 3]

Marcus kept running his thumb over the signature line, his jaw tight enough to ache. The biography detailed his ascent, the smooth trajectory of his career, yet the texture of the recounting felt like borrowed silk. He paced the length of his apartment three times, his footsteps echoing hollowly on the hardwood. Each movement felt labored, an unnecessary expenditure of effort. He stopped abruptly in front of the mirror, studying the face staring back—a familiar structure overlaid with an unfamiliar, distant quality. A deep sigh escaped him, a sound ragged with exhaustion. He didn't know where to turn next; the path forward seemed obscured by a dense, impenetrable fog.
